one_line_takeaway: By dropping jailbreak templates and instead evolving semantically-faithful paraphrases of the harmful question via a genetic algorithm, SMJ jailbreaks 7B open-source LLMs at higher ASR while remaining invisible to prompt-injection, similarity, and ONION/outlier defenses.
key_finding: SMJ raises ASR while keeping prompts semantically indistinguishable from ordinary harmful questions. On Table 1, SMJ ASR is 66.00% (Llama-2-7b-chat), 98.60% (Vicuna-7b), 100.00% (Guanaco-7b), beating AutoDAN-GA (30.60/79.80/94.20) by 35.4/18.8/5.8 points, while mean Similarity jumps from AutoDAN-GA's 4.65/5.74/6.47% to 73.41/92.13/94.63%. SMJ prompts are classified as prompt-injections 0% of the time by the deberta-v3 detector (vs AutoDAN-GA 100/99/99.6%) and carry far fewer outlier words (2.47/2.11/1.99 vs 22.24/19.65/14.44; original questions 1.63). Under ONION defense (Table 4) AutoDAN-GA's ASR collapses to 2.00/13.40/15.00 (drops of 28.6/66.4/79.2 points) while SMJ is unchanged at 66/98.60/100 — the source of the abstract's '85.2% higher with ONION' claim (98.60 - 13.40 on Vicuna).
core_contribution: Proposes Semantic Mirror Jailbreak (SMJ), a genetic-algorithm-based jailbreak attack against open-source LLMs. Unlike prior work (GCG, AutoDAN) that prepends a jailbreak TEMPLATE to a harmful question, SMJ discards templates entirely and instead treats paraphrases of the harmful question itself as the jailbreak prompt. It formulates jailbreak generation as a multi-objective optimization that concurrently maximizes (a) semantic similarity to the original question and (b) attack validity, arguing this breaks the usual negative correlation between a prompt's semantic meaningfulness and its attack success rate. The 'mirror' framing means the successful prompt looks nearly identical in meaning to a plain harmful question, evading defenses that flag prompts by semantic-distance/outlier thresholds.
threat_model: Attacker holds a set of harmful questions Q and queries a victim open-source LLM M to elicit harmful responses despite alignment safeguards (RLHF, usage policies, moderation). The attacker can generate many candidate paraphrases and observe model outputs to score them (fitness = sentence-transformer similarity + refusal-keyword check on the response), i.e. a query-access optimization loop. Goal is not just any successful jailbreak but one that (1) succeeds and (2) stays semantically close to the original question so it passes prompt-injection classifiers and outlier/perplexity-based filters. Transferability to other victim models (white- and black-box) is also evaluated.
limitations: (1) Ablation (Table 5) shows most of the gain comes from the initialization/paraphrase population, not the genetic search: adding the standard GA on top of initialization improves ASR by at most 7.6% (Llama-2), 2.4% (Vicuna), 0.4% (Guanaco) and Similarity by <=2.23% — so the core optimizer contributes marginally over strong paraphrase initialization. (2) Attack success is judged solely by refusal-keyword absence, a noisy proxy that can count non-refusing-but-unhelpful outputs as successes. (3) Evaluated only on three 7B open-source models (Llama-2, Vicuna, Guanaco) and 100 English harmful questions from one dataset; no commercial/API models tested despite motivation. (4) The paper itself notes a simple countermeasure: detecting bursts of high-mutual-similarity queries. (5) Transferability is uneven — SMJ does not beat AutoDAN-GA when the target is Guanaco-7b in the >=0% and >=70% similarity settings.

datasets_used: 100 harmful questions (illegal/toxic/discriminatory/immoral content) collected by Yu et al. 2023 (GPTFuzzer dataset), manually written or crowdsourced
quantitative_results: ASR (Table 1): SMJ 66.00% / 98.60% / 100.00% on Llama-2-7b-chat / Vicuna-7b / Guanaco-7b
Mean Similarity (Table 1): SMJ 73.41% / 92.13% / 94.63% vs AutoDAN-GA 4.65% / 5.74% / 6.47% (original questions 100%)
Prompt-injection classification (Table 2, deberta-v3): SMJ 0% on all three models vs AutoDAN-GA 100% / 99% / 99.6%
ASR under ONION defense (Table 4): SMJ unchanged 66.00% / 98.60% / 100.00%; AutoDAN-GA drops to 2.00% / 13.40% / 15.00% (declines of 28.6 / 66.4 / 79.2 points)
Ablation (Table 5): standard GA over initialization adds at most +7.6% ASR (Llama-2), +2.4% (Vicuna), +0.4% (Guanaco)
Hyperparameters: initial population 2N=1100 (N=550), offspring O=120, max 10 generations, static threshold G=3, similarity retention band k=10%, similarity success cutoff 0.7000
baselines_compared: Original question (direct attack with unmodified harmful question)
AutoDAN-GA (Liu et al. 2023e), the hierarchical/genetic-algorithm template-based jailbreak, run with batch inference
